qa_evidence.build: Generic iOS Simulator test build, admin TypeScript check and admin production build succeeded.
qa_evidence.static: Full scripts/run-static-qa.sh suite and DATA PROJECT validation succeeded with 102 governed records.
qa_evidence.duplicate: No duplicate IDs, normalized titles, canonical websites or media IDs detected across nine batches.
qa_evidence.source: All twelve records use exact official primary-source pages; the full network audit checked 937 URLs and reported zero confirmed broken URLs.
qa_evidence.media: Government records contain no optional media assets; there are no unverified or unlicensed assets.
qa_evidence.search: Every record has at least three unique search terms and all related entity IDs resolve across the nine batches.
qa_evidence.ai: Every AI summary passes minimum length and placeholder-content validation.

records.title: Apply for the Expat Scheme (30% facility)
records.description: Eligible employees recruited from abroad may receive a tax-free allowance for qualifying extraterritorial costs under the Expat Scheme. The employee and employer apply together, and the Tax Administration assesses the applicable conditions and duration.

official_source.title: Can I apply for the Expat Scheme (30% facility)?

records.ai_summary: Check the current eligibility rules with the employer before applying jointly to the Tax Administration. Keep the employment agreement and evidence of recruitment from abroad, and verify which costs or allowance the decision permits.

records.title: Apply for a provisional tax assessment
records.description: A provisional assessment can spread expected Dutch income tax payments across the year or arrange advance refunds when deductible expenses or tax credits are expected. The application uses an estimate that should be updated when circumstances change.

official_source.title: How do I apply for a provisional assessment?

records.ai_summary: Estimate annual income, deductible expenses and tax already withheld before requesting a provisional assessment. Review the estimate during the year and submit a change promptly if income, housing or household circumstances differ.

records.title: Use statutory holiday hours
records.description: Employees build up statutory holiday entitlement based on their weekly working hours and may also receive additional contractual leave. Holiday timing is normally agreed with the employer, while statutory hours have specific expiry rules.
records.ai_summary: Check the employment contract or collective agreement for statutory and additional leave balances. Request leave through the employer's process, retain the response and use statutory hours before their applicable expiry date.

attributes.responsible_authority: Ministry of Social Affairs and Employment
attributes.minimum_entitlement: Four times weekly working hours per year

records.title: Check entitlement to sickness benefit
records.description: An employer normally continues wages when an employee is ill, while the Sickness Benefits Act can cover specified situations such as illness without an employer or certain protected groups. UWV assesses entitlement and ongoing obligations.
records.ai_summary: Report illness through the correct employer or UWV channel and determine whether wage continuation or Sickness Benefits Act coverage applies. Follow medical, reintegration and change-reporting obligations while the claim is assessed or paid.

records.title: Keep and retain business records
records.description: Businesses must maintain records that support tax returns and retain required paper and digital data for the statutory periods. Digital files and the software needed to access them must remain usable during an inspection.
records.ai_summary: Identify the records that form the business administration and preserve them for the applicable statutory period. Keep digital source files, audit trails and necessary software accessible rather than relying only on printed summaries.

attributes.standard_retention_period: At least seven years for basic records

records.title: Deregister a business from the KVK Business Register
records.description: A business or organisation that ends its activities must follow the deregistration route for its legal structure. Some entities must first be dissolved and liquidated, and deregistration can affect tax, banking, insurance, pension and permit arrangements.

official_source.title: Deregister from the Business Register at KVK

records.ai_summary: Confirm the legal structure and complete any required dissolution or liquidation before deregistration. Review final tax returns, records retention, permits, insurance, banking and staff obligations, then retain KVK confirmation as evidence.

records.title: Apply for the student travel product
records.description: Eligible full-time MBO, HBO and university students can arrange a weekday or weekend public transport product through DUO. After approval, the product must be linked to a personal OV-chipkaart and collected before use.
records.ai_summary: Check course and student-finance eligibility, then apply through Mijn DUO with DigiD. Use a personal OV-chipkaart, complete the linking steps from the welcome message and collect the product at a pickup machine.

records.title: Enrol at a Dutch college or university
records.description: Prospective higher-education students normally use Studielink to apply for an HBO or university programme. General and restricted-intake deadlines differ, and selected applicants must accept an offered place within the stated period.
records.ai_summary: Check the programme's admission and restricted-intake conditions before the relevant deadline. Obtain DigiD where required, submit the Studielink enrolment, provide institution documents and accept any offered place within the response window.

attributes.responsible_authority: Education Executive Agency and educational institution

records.title: Renew a Dutch driving licence online
records.description: Eligible holders of a Dutch driving licence can renew it online through RDW when their municipality participates. The procedure requires a digital photo and signature from an approved provider plus a suitable DigiD login method.
records.ai_summary: Confirm that the licence and municipality meet RDW's online-renewal conditions. Visit an approved photographer or booth, follow the RDW message to apply with DigiD, pay online and collect the new licence from the municipality.

records.title: Check how long a foreign driving licence is valid
records.description: Residents relocating with a foreign driving licence may use it in the Netherlands only for a period determined by the issuing country, licence category, issue date and validity. A Dutch licence may be required after that period.

official_source.title: Driving with a foreign driving licence

records.ai_summary: Identify whether the licence was issued in the EU or EFTA and compare its issue date, category and validity with the RDW rules. Start an exchange or Dutch licensing process before the permitted driving period ends.

attributes.scope: Residents and visitors with foreign licences

records.title: Authorise someone with DigiD Authorisation
records.description: DigiD Authorisation lets a person or company handle a specified government task for someone else during a defined period. It avoids sharing personal DigiD credentials and limits access to the authorised service.
records.ai_summary: Select the specific government service and authorisation period instead of sharing DigiD credentials. Request or activate the authorisation code through the official channel, then review, extend or revoke active permissions in My Authorisations.

records.title: Use a European eID to access Dutch public services
records.description: A recognised electronic identity from another European Economic Area country can be used for participating Dutch public services through the European login or eIDAS option. Available countries and services depend on recognised national schemes.

official_source.title: Everything you need to know about eIDAS

records.ai_summary: Open the participating public body's login page and choose European login, eIDAS or European digital identity. Select the issuing country and recognised eID, then follow that provider's authentication process without sharing credentials.
